channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ransom claims: I’ve never seen N1bn cash, let alone N5bn, says Ndume
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Senator Ali Ndume vehemently denies receiving N5bn ransom or negotiating with kidnappers, urging security agencies to investigate the serious allegations.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ransom-claims-ive-never-seen-n1bn-cash-let-alone-n5bn-says-ndume/

item.title: NUT plans protest over alleged move to divide union in Delta
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) in Delta State plans a protest over alleged attempts to divide the union and undermine a 2018 agreement. Learn more abo
Read More: https://punchng.com/nut-plans-protest-over-alleged-move-to-divide-union-in-delta/

item.title: Lagos introduces compulsory building insurance to boost safety
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State launches a compulsory building insurance scheme to boost safety, protect lives and property, and ensure accountability in the construction sect
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-introduces-compulsory-building-insurance-to-boost-safety/

item.title: Israel, Nigeria strengthen research, technology collaboration
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria and Israel deepen collaboration in research, technology, and innovation, expanding partnerships to drive entrepreneurship and economic development.
Read More: https://punchng.com/israel-nigeria-strengthen-research-technology-collaboration/

item.title: US court rules abortion pill restrictions unlawful
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A US federal court has ruled that restrictions on the abortion pill mifepristone are “unlawful,” ordering the Trump administration to reconsider the rules.
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-court-rules-abortion-pill-restrictions-unlawful/

item.title: Oil Prices Volatile as Supply Disruptions Hit Four Global Fronts
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brent futures surged past $100 a barrel as the conflict in the Middle East widens and Ukraine strikes Russian infrastructure.

item.title: ‘There’s Nowhere Like Home,’ Tinubu Urges Healthcare Professionals In Diaspora To Return
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The President acknowledged that Nigeria needs the knowledge, innovation, experience and patriotism of its highly skilled professionals abroad to build a resilient and world-class healthcare system for all citizens. The post ‘There’s Nowhere Like Home,’ Tinubu Urges Healthcare Professionals In

item.title: Ekiti 2026: APC hails Supreme Court judgment affirming Oyebanji’s candidacy
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ekiti APC celebrates Supreme Court’s affirmation of Governor Oyebanji’s candidacy for the 2026 governorship election, calling it a victory for democracy.

item.title: Done Deal: Danish Club FC Midtylland Sign Nigerian Youngster
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Danish club FC Midtylland have completed the signing of Nigerian midfielder, Hassan Momodu Kashietu, Completesports.com reports. Kashietu joins the Black and Reds from Nigerian club FC Ebedei. The 18-year-old midfielder will become part of FC Midtjylland’s U-19 team. Kashietu Eager To Impress

item.title: I’m 38, lost my job, and have to move back in with my parents. What did I do wrong?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: My emergency fund is running out — I thought I did everything right

item.title: CAN Rejects ₦50,000 Fine For Preaching In Commercial Buses, Urges Tinubu To Reject Bill
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), South-West zone, has kicked against the proposed ₦50,000 fine for preaching in commercial vehicles, describing the provision as a possible violation of Nigerians’ constitutional rights. The reaction followed the passage of the Federal Road Safety Corps (Am

item.title: NAOWA, Group Train 100 Women, Youths In Edo
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Army Officers’ Wives Association (NAOWA), 4 Brigade Chapter, in collaboration with the Ocean Wave Group, has inaugurated a two-month skills acquisition programme for 100 women and youths in the Ekehuan Military Cantonment, Benin City, Edo State. The training, which commenced on Ju

item.title: Abia Govt Begins Transformer Distribution Across Rural Communities
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Abia State Government has commenced the first phase of the distribution of 50 electricity transformers to communities across the 17 Local Government Areas of the state as part of efforts to improve electricity supply and strengthen power infrastructure, particularly in rural areas. The initiativ

item.title: Asiri Eniba’s appointment as NURTW chairman null and void — Osun govt
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Osun State Government has declared the purported appointment of Kazeem Oyewale, also known as Asiri Eniba, as Chairman of The post Asiri Eniba’s appointment as NURTW chairman null and void — Osun govt appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Crackdown: Lagos taskforce arrests 46, recovers drugs, weapon
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni Operatives of the Lagos State Taskforce have arrested 46 suspects and recovered suspected illicit drugs, a locally made gun and other dangerous items during a series of enforcement operations across the state. The agency said the operation, which commenced at about 9 a.m. on Fri

item.title: Ondo Police Arrest Four Over Alleged Jungle Justice, Viral Humiliation Video
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested four suspects over the alleged assault and public humiliation of a resident whose video was reportedly recorded without his consent and circulated on social media. Police spokesperson said the complainant, Boluwaji Temidayo, reported the incident at the Oku

item.title: Salis visits Atiku, to meet Tinubu, Obasanjo over national development
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: West Africa’s first astronaut, Chief Owolabi Salis, has met former Vice President Atiku Abubakar as part of a strategic dialogue targeted at national development. The New York-based lawyer and chartered accountant, who was in Nigeria recently, disclosed that the move had become expedient to put Nige

item.title: Oborevwori rewards Delta student with N20m for winning World Spelling Bee Championship
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Delta State Governor, Rt. Hon. Sheriff Oborevwori, has rewarded Master Great Chinedu Okediashi with N20 million for emerging as the winner of the World Spelling Bee Championship in China, describing the feat as a testament to the state’s sustained investment in education. The governor announce

item.title: Abdulsalami To INEC Chairman: Try To Win Confidence Of Politicians

item.description: Former head of state, General Abdulsalami Abubakar (rtd.) has advised the Chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), Prof. Joash Amupitan, SAN, to try to win the confidence of politicians. Abubakar, who spoke on Friday when he received the INEC team in his Minna residence, war

item.title: Enugu Air: Gov. Mbah Reacts To Runway Incident, Says Safety Non-negotiable
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Governor of Enugu State, Dr. Peter Mbah, has reacted to Thursday’s runway incident involving the state-owned airline, Enugu Air, at Benin Airport, empathizing with the passengers and crew members over the traumatic experience. Mbah, in a statement he personally signed, commended the aviati

item.title: ICC top prosecutor removed over sexual misconduct allegations
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Karim Khan denies allegations of engaging in an improper sexual relationship with a junior ICC staff member and seeking to stop her pursuing her complaint.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Chairman/Chief Executive Officer of the Nigerians in Diaspora Commission, NiDCOM, Mrs. Abike Dabiri-Erewa, on Friday called on the Federal Government to demand compensation for Nigerians who were forced to abandon their jobs, businesses and property in South Africa before returning home. The pos

item.title: Volkswagen engineers charged with insider trading tied to Rivian joint venture
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The indictment, which was unsealed Friday, alleges the Volkswagen engineers used confidential insider information to buy stock in Rivian.

item.title: Thieves snatch 2500-year-old gold necklace from French museum
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Thieves raided a museum in eastern France in broad daylight Friday, making off with a gold necklace dating back 2,500 years, prosecutors said. The post Thieves snatch 2500-year-old gold necklace from French museum appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Glasgow 2026: Oluwafemiayo Smashes World Record, Extends Team Nigeria’s Medal Haul
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Folashade Oluwafemiayo delivered another dominant display to win the gold medal in the women’s heavyweight para powerlifting event at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. The reigning world champion shattered her own world record with a sensational lift of 175kg, finishing w

item.title: US Cattle Herd Grows Slightly in Potential Sign of Rebuilding
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US cattle herd grew modestly from the prior year, in a signal that ranchers may be starting to retain more animals following a historic shortage.

item.title: Osun 2026: PAACA, NPF, CSOs, Others Seek Responsible Social Media Use, Peaceful Election

item.description: As preparations intensify for the August 15 Osun State governorship election, the National Orientation Agency (NOA), the Nigeria Police Force (NPF) and Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) have called on residents to embrace peaceful conduct, reject misinformation and use social media responsibly to s

item.title: Hedge Funds Turn Most Negative on Canadian Dollar Since 2024
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Hedge funds have accumulated the most negative bets on the Canadian dollar in two years as the threat of American tariffs drags on the currency.

item.title: 22 Sokoto Indigenes Head To India, Pakistan On Govt Scholarship
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Sokoto State Government has sent 22 indigenes to study professional courses at various universities in India and Pakistan. Under the new arrangement, the 22 qualified students will study courses including Cybersecurity, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Engineering, and Radiology, among others.

The party

item.title: Police Hand Over Recovered Vehicle to Owner 5 Years After Theft
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Gombe State Police Command has handed over a Toyota Highlander recovered in the state to its rightful owner, Mrs Cecilia A. Duru of Port Harcourt, Rivers State, nearly five years after the vehicle was stolen. The vehicle was presented to its owner on Friday after police concluded investigations

item.title: 2025 AFCON: CAS fixes October 8 to decide Senegal, Morocco title dispute
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) has fixed October 8 as the date to hear the case over the winner of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON).

item.title: Nigeria wins gold at Commonwealth Games
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Nigeria have made a splash on the first full day of the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth games with a record-breaking performance by Esther Nworgu in the women’s para-weightlifting event on Friday. Nworgu won the gold medal of the women’s Lightweight para-weightlifting event with a record 119 points to cla

item.dc:creator: TechEconomy

Some of the best conversions happen when businesses step into their customers’ shoes. A story lands

item.title: Phoenix skittled for just 64 as Rockets earn thumping win at Edgbaston
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Birmingham Phoenix were skittled for just 64 by Trent Rockets as they slumped to a resounding nine-wicket loss at Edgbaston in the women's competition in The Hundred.

item.description: James, a four-time NBA Most Valuable Player, has won four titles in a 23-year career, most recently with the Los Angeles Lakers. The post LeBron James Joins Philadelphia 76ers, Eyes One Last NBA Title appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Meta just created a moderation nightmare for its smart glasses
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Meta's smart glasses have been a PR headache for the company. Public backlash has been swift, and fierce; people are concerned about the erosion of privacy and expansion of surveillance. Some especially bad actors are using the glasses to film themselves "pranking" random strangers. Women have becom

item.title: Police Probe Four Officers Over Ethnic Profiling Claims

item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested four officers over unprofessional and ethnically insensitive remarks made to a motorist during a stop-and-search operation along the Lagos–Benin Expressway. In a viral video, the officers confronted a UK returnee who challenged their attempt to stop and sea

item.title: Fury Defeats Wach, Closes In On Anthony Joshua Fight
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Tyson Fury edged closer to a blockbuster heavyweight clash with Anthony Joshua after defeating Mariusz Wach by technical knock-out in the Thai resort city of Pattaya on Friday. The 37-year-old Briton outclassed his Polish opponent in front of 1,500 spectators in a bout that was not broadcast live on

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Fitch Ratings upgraded Jane Street Group to investment-grade status on Friday, citing the market maker’s strong income growth throughout the economic cycle.

item.title: Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY actually scales
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.dbos.dev/blog/postgres-listen-notify-scalability Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49040296 Points: 55 # Comments: 6

item.title: Political Violence: NANS Calls For Temporary Shutdown Of Osun Campuses
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS), Southwest Zone D, has called for the temporary suspension of academic activities in tertiary institutions across Osun State ahead of the August 15 governorship election, citing growing concerns over political violence and the safety of students.

item.title: Oil to End Year Near $100 on Mideast Disruptions, Rapidan Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The breakdown of the US-Iran ceasefire threatens to leave Brent crude hovering around the $100-a-barrel mark well past July and into the end of the year, according to Rapidan Energy Group’s increased forecast.

item.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Price regains lead over Smith in epic QF
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Fury not convinced Joshua will 'have the guts' to fight him
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Tyson Fury remains unconvinced that Anthony Joshua will agree to a long-awaited blockbuster British showdown, questioning whether his rival has the "guts" to face him.

item.title: ‘I’m still confused’: I sold $80,000 in shares on June 30, so why didn’t I receive my dividend?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “To my surprise, the proceeds from the sale were credited to my money-market settlement fund.”

item.title: Nigeria, Israel strengthen innovation ecosystem with fresh research investment
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria and Israel have strengthened their partnership in research, innovation and entrepreneurship with fresh investment in The post Nigeria, Israel strengthen innovation ecosystem with fresh research investment appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: LSEG Reviews US Investment Grade Fund Flow Data After JPMorgan Flags Error
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: LSEG Lipper is reviewing weekly data showing US investment grade funds saw the biggest outflows in more than six years after a report published by JPMorgan Chase & Co. said there was a potential error in Lipper’s data.

item.title: The stock market is completely unprepared for a 6% yield on the 30-year Treasury
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Long-bond spike would crush stock gains and deepen bond-fund losses.

item.title: Trump vows to investigate EU over fining of US tech companies
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US president says fines against Google, as well as Apple, Meta and Amazon, should be "entirely reversed."

item.title: Tyson Fury sets up potential Anthony Joshua clash with dominant TKO victory
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Tyson Fury edged closer to a blockbuster heavyweight clash with Anthony Joshua after defeating Poland's Mariusz Wach by technical knock-out on Friday, but said he was "not convinced" his fellow Briton would face him. The post Tyson Fury sets up potential Anthony Joshua clash with dominant TKO victor

item.title: Heirs Holdings portfolio companies earn international recognition across industries
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Transcorp Group, Abuja Electricity Distribution Company (AEDC) and Redtech, three Heirs Holdings portfolio companies - have earned recognition in leading global and continental business rankings published by The Africa Report and CNBC The post Heirs Holdings portfolio companies earn international re

item.title: 2027: Your Votes Will Count, INEC Chairman Assures Nigerians
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), Prof. Joash Amupitan, has assured Nigerians that their votes will count in the 2027 general elections. Amupitan gave the assurance during a courtesy visit to former Head of State and Chairman of the National Peace Committee, Gener

item.title: 🔴 Live: More than 100,000 evacuated in France's fire-hit southwest, PM Lecornu calls crisis meeting
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Aptech career quest 2026 returns with up to 50% scholarship opportunities for UK degree aspirants
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: This premier career seminar, Nigeria’s largest of its kind, is set to take place this August in major cities including Lagos, Abuja, Enugu, Ibadan, Port Harcourt, and Calabar The post Aptech career quest 2026 returns with up to 50% scholarship opportunities for UK degree aspirants appeared first on

item.title: NITDA Targets Skilled Tech Workforce to Modernize Nigeria’s Public Sector
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: The National Information Technology Development Agency has reiterated the need for a digitally skilled public sector workforce, saying the success of government reforms and the adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) will depend largely on the digital capacity of civil servants. Kashifu Inuwa Abdul
